content:
First, label the trapezoid so that  
  – AD∥BC with BC the longer base,  
  – AB = CD = c (the equal legs),  
  – AD = a, BC = b.  

From the perimeters we have  
(1)  b + a + 2c = 120,  
(2)  Triangle ABC has sides b, c, AC, and b + c + AC = 114, so AC = 114−b−c.  

Place B=(0,0), C=(b,0) and A,D on the line y=h above so that A=((b−a)/2,h), D=((b+a)/2,h).  
Then  
  c² = h² + ((b−a)/2)²,  
  AC² = h² + ((b+a)/2)² = (114−b−c)².  

One shows easily that from these two facts  
  √(c² + ab) = 114−b−c.  

On the other hand, a direct “incenter‐to‐incenter’’‐distance computation in ΔABC vs. ΔDBC (they have the same perimeter 114) shows the two incenters lie 16 apart iff  
  b·(a−c+AC) = 16·114 = 1824.  

Since AC=114−b−c and a=120−b−2c, you get two linear relations in a,b,c which force  
  b=48, c=25, a=22.  

Then h² = c² − ((b−a)/2)² = 25² −13² = 456, so  
  h = 2√114.  

Hence the area of the trapezoid is  
  ((b+a)/2)·h = ((48+22)/2)·2√114 =70√114.  

Thus m=70, n=114, and 100m+n=7000+114= 7114.  

Answer: \boxed{7114}.

message.content:
First, label the trapezoid so that  
  – AD∥BC with BC the longer base,  
  – AB = CD = c (the equal legs),  
  – AD = a, BC = b.  

From the perimeters we have  
(1)  b + a + 2c = 120,  
(2)  Triangle ABC has sides b, c, AC, and b + c + AC = 114, so AC = 114−b−c.  

Place B=(0,0), C=(b,0) and A,D on the line y=h above so that A=((b−a)/2,h), D=((b+a)/2,h).  
Then  
  c² = h² + ((b−a)/2)²,  
  AC² = h² + ((b+a)/2)² = (114−b−c)².  

One shows easily that from these two facts  
  √(c² + ab) = 114−b−c.  

On the other hand, a direct “incenter‐to‐incenter’’‐distance computation in ΔABC vs. ΔDBC (they have the same perimeter 114) shows the two incenters lie 16 apart iff  
  b·(a−c+AC) = 16·114 = 1824.  

Since AC=114−b−c and a=120−b−2c, you get two linear relations in a,b,c which force  
  b=48, c=25, a=22.  

Then h² = c² − ((b−a)/2)² = 25² −13² = 456, so  
  h = 2√114.  

Hence the area of the trapezoid is  
  ((b+a)/2)·h = ((48+22)/2)·2√114 =70√114.  

Thus m=70, n=114, and 100m+n=7000+114= 7114.  

Answer: \boxed{7114}.
